tasks.title: Initialize monorepo structure and workspace configuration
tasks.description: Set up the project foundation with proper workspace structure, package.json configuration, and development scripts
tasks.details: Create root package.json with workspaces configuration for client/ and server/ directories. Set up npm scripts: 'dev' (runs both client and server concurrently), 'build' (builds client for production), 'start' (serves production build). Install concurrently for parallel script execution. Create basic directory structure: kanban/client/, kanban/server/, kanban/server/db/, kanban/server/api/, kanban/server/ws/, kanban/client/src/. Initialize package.json files in both workspaces.
tasks.testStrategy: Verify npm run dev starts both processes, npm run build completes without errors, and directory structure matches specification

subtasks.title: Create root package.json with workspace configuration
subtasks.description: Set up the main package.json file with npm workspaces configuration for client and server directories
subtasks.details: Create root package.json with workspaces array containing 'client' and 'server'. Set private: true, name: 'kanban-app', and version: '1.0.0'. Configure workspaces to enable npm workspace commands for managing dependencies across the monorepo.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify npm workspace commands work correctly and both client and server are recognized as workspaces

subtasks.title: Set up npm scripts for development and production
subtasks.description: Configure npm scripts in root package.json for concurrent development, building, and production serving
subtasks.details: Add npm scripts: 'dev' using concurrently to run both client and server in parallel, 'build' to build client for production, 'start' to serve production build. Install concurrently package as devDependency. Configure scripts to handle cross-platform compatibility.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test npm run dev starts both processes concurrently, npm run build completes successfully, and npm run start serves the application

subtasks.title: Create directory structure for client and server
subtasks.description: Establish the complete folder hierarchy for both client and server workspaces with all required subdirectories
subtasks.details: Create directory structure: kanban/client/, kanban/server/, kanban/server/db/, kanban/server/api/, kanban/server/ws/, kanban/client/src/. Ensure proper permissions and that all directories are created recursively. Add .gitkeep files where necessary to maintain empty directories in version control.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify all directories exist with correct structure and are accessible for file operations

subtasks.title: Initialize individual workspace package.json files
subtasks.description: Create separate package.json files for client and server workspaces with appropriate configurations
subtasks.details: Create client/package.json with React/Vite configuration, name: 'kanban-client', scripts for dev/build/preview. Create server/package.json with Node.js configuration, name: 'kanban-server', scripts for dev/start. Set appropriate main entry points and ensure workspace dependencies are properly configured.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify both package.json files are valid JSON, contain required fields, and workspace commands can target individual packages

tasks.expansionPrompt: Break down the monorepo initialization into: 1) Create root package.json with workspace configuration, 2) Set up npm scripts for development and production, 3) Create directory structure for client and server, 4) Initialize individual workspace package.json files

tasks.title: Setup Vite + React client application
tasks.description: Initialize the React frontend with Vite build tool and basic routing configuration
tasks.details: Run 'npm create vite@latest client -- --template react' in the client directory. Configure vite.config.js with proxy settings for /api and /ws endpoints to localhost:3001. Set up basic App.jsx component structure. Install required dependencies: react, react-dom, @vitejs/plugin-react. Configure index.html with proper meta tags and title. Ensure HMR works correctly.
tasks.testStrategy: Verify localhost:5173 serves React app without errors, HMR updates work, and proxy configuration is ready for API calls

subtasks.title: Initialize Vite React project and install dependencies
subtasks.description: Create new Vite React project using the official template and install all required dependencies
subtasks.details: Run 'npm create vite@latest client -- --template react' in the client directory. Install required dependencies: react, react-dom, @vitejs/plugin-react. Clean up default template files and prepare project structure for custom implementation.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify project creates successfully, all dependencies install without errors, and basic Vite dev server starts on localhost:5173

subtasks.title: Configure vite.config.js with proxy settings for API endpoints
subtasks.description: Set up Vite configuration file with proxy settings to route API and WebSocket calls to the backend server
subtasks.details: Configure vite.config.js with proxy settings for /api and /ws endpoints to localhost:3001. Set up proper proxy configuration to handle both REST API calls and WebSocket connections. Ensure CORS and development server settings are properly configured.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test that proxy configuration routes requests correctly by making test calls to /api endpoints and verifying they reach localhost:3001

subtasks.title: Set up basic App.jsx structure and verify HMR functionality
subtasks.description: Create the main App component structure and configure index.html with proper meta tags, then verify Hot Module Replacement works correctly
subtasks.details: Set up basic App.jsx component structure with placeholder content. Configure index.html with proper meta tags, title, and viewport settings. Test Hot Module Replacement (HMR) functionality to ensure development workflow is smooth and changes reflect immediately.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify localhost:5173 serves React app without errors, make changes to App.jsx and confirm HMR updates work instantly, check console for any errors

tasks.expansionPrompt: Split into: 1) Initialize Vite React project and install dependencies, 2) Configure vite.config.js with proxy settings for API endpoints, 3) Set up basic App.jsx structure and verify HMR functionality

tasks.title: Setup Express server with health endpoint
tasks.description: Create the Node.js Express server with basic routing and health check endpoint
tasks.details: Create server/index.js with Express app setup. Install dependencies: express, cors, better-sqlite3, ws, uuid, nodemon (dev). Configure CORS for development. Add GET /health endpoint returning {ok: true}. Set up nodemon for development with proper file watching. Configure Express to serve static files from client/dist in production mode. Add error handling middleware.
tasks.testStrategy: Verify GET /health returns 200 with {ok: true}, server restarts on file changes in dev mode, and CORS headers are present

subtasks.title: Create basic Express app with dependencies installation
subtasks.description: Set up the foundational Express application structure and install all required dependencies for the server
subtasks.details: Create server/index.js file with basic Express app initialization. Install required dependencies using npm: express, cors, better-sqlite3, ws, uuid, and nodemon as dev dependency. Initialize package.json if not exists and configure basic npm scripts for development and production.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify all dependencies are installed correctly and Express app can be imported without errors

subtasks.title: Configure CORS and middleware setup
subtasks.description: Set up CORS configuration for development environment and configure essential Express middleware
subtasks.details: Configure CORS middleware to allow cross-origin requests during development. Set up Express JSON parsing middleware for handling request bodies. Configure any additional middleware needed for request processing and logging.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test CORS headers are present in responses and JSON request bodies are parsed correctly

subtasks.title: Implement health endpoint and error handling
subtasks.description: Create the health check endpoint and implement comprehensive error handling middleware
subtasks.details: Add GET /health endpoint that returns {ok: true} with 200 status code. Implement error handling middleware to catch and properly format errors with appropriate HTTP status codes. Ensure proper error responses for different types of failures.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify GET /health returns 200 status with {ok: true} response and error handling middleware catches and formats errors properly

subtasks.title: Set up nodemon for development and static file serving
subtasks.description: Configure development environment with nodemon and production static file serving capabilities
subtasks.details: Configure nodemon for development with proper file watching patterns and restart behavior. Set up Express to serve static files from client/dist directory in production mode. Add environment-specific configuration to distinguish between development and production modes.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test server restarts automatically on file changes in development mode and static files are served correctly in production mode

tasks.expansionPrompt: Break down into: 1) Create basic Express app with dependencies installation, 2) Configure CORS and middleware setup, 3) Implement health endpoint and error handling, 4) Set up nodemon for development and static file serving

tasks.title: Create database schema and migration system
tasks.description: Design SQLite database schema for cards and comments with migration runner
tasks.details: Create server/db/migrations/001_init.sql with cards table (id TEXT PRIMARY KEY, title TEXT NOT NULL, assignee TEXT, column TEXT DEFAULT 'ready', position REAL NOT NULL, description TEXT, created_at INTEGER NOT NULL) and comments table (id TEXT PRIMARY KEY, card_id TEXT REFERENCES cards(id) ON DELETE CASCADE, author TEXT NOT NULL, content TEXT NOT NULL, created_at INTEGER NOT NULL). Create server/db/schema.js migration runner that checks schema_version table and applies pending migrations on startup. Add indexes for performance on card_id and column fields.
tasks.testStrategy: Verify migration creates kanban.db file with correct tables and indexes, migration runner applies only new migrations on subsequent runs

subtasks.title: Design and create SQL schema files for cards and comments tables
subtasks.description: Create the initial SQL migration file with properly structured tables and relationships for the kanban board database
subtasks.details: Create server/db/migrations/001_init.sql file containing SQL DDL statements for cards table (id TEXT PRIMARY KEY, title TEXT NOT NULL, assignee TEXT, column TEXT DEFAULT 'ready', position REAL NOT NULL, description TEXT, created_at INTEGER NOT NULL) and comments table (id TEXT PRIMARY KEY, card_id TEXT REFERENCES cards(id) ON DELETE CASCADE, author TEXT NOT NULL, content TEXT NOT NULL, created_at INTEGER NOT NULL). Ensure proper foreign key constraints and data types are defined.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify SQL syntax is valid, tables can be created successfully, foreign key constraints work properly, and default values are applied correctly

subtasks.title: Implement migration runner system with version tracking
subtasks.description: Build the migration system that tracks applied migrations and executes pending ones automatically
subtasks.details: Create server/db/schema.js with migration runner functionality that maintains a schema_version table to track applied migrations. Implement logic to scan migrations directory, compare against applied migrations, and execute pending migrations in order. Add error handling for failed migrations and ensure atomic execution. Include startup integration to run migrations automatically when server starts.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test migration runner applies new migrations only once, tracks version correctly, handles migration failures gracefully, and works on fresh database and existing database

subtasks.title: Add database indexes and test migration execution
subtasks.description: Create performance indexes and thoroughly test the complete migration system functionality
subtasks.details: Add CREATE INDEX statements to the migration file for card_id field in comments table and column field in cards table to optimize query performance. Test the complete migration system by running it on fresh database and verifying all tables, constraints, and indexes are created correctly. Verify the kanban.db file is generated with proper schema structure.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify indexes are created and improve query performance, test migration creates kanban.db file with correct tables and indexes, confirm migration runner applies only new migrations on subsequent runs

tasks.expansionPrompt: Split into: 1) Design and create SQL schema files for cards and comments tables with proper relationships, 2) Implement migration runner system with version tracking, 3) Add database indexes and test migration execution

tasks.title: Implement database query functions
tasks.description: Create all SQLite query functions for CRUD operations on cards and comments
tasks.details: Create server/db/queries.js with functions: initDb() (opens DB and runs migrations), getCards() (returns all cards with nested comments ordered by column and position), createCard(data) (inserts card with UUID, returns created card), updateCard(id, data) (partial update using Object.keys), deleteCard(id) (deletes card and cascades comments), moveCard(id, column, position) (updates position and reorders siblings using fractional indexing), createComment(cardId, data) (inserts comment with UUID and timestamp). Use better-sqlite3 prepared statements for performance.
tasks.testStrategy: Unit tests for each function using in-memory SQLite database, verify cascade deletes work, test position reordering logic

subtasks.title: Implement basic CRUD operations for cards
subtasks.description: Create fundamental database operations for card management including create, read, update, and delete functions
subtasks.details: Implement initDb(), getCards(), createCard(), updateCard(), and deleteCard() functions in server/db/queries.js. Use better-sqlite3 for database operations. Include UUID generation for new cards and proper error handling for each operation.
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit tests for each CRUD function using in-memory SQLite database, verify data integrity and proper return values

subtasks.title: Implement complex moveCard function with fractional indexing
subtasks.description: Create sophisticated card positioning system using fractional indexing for smooth reordering
subtasks.details: Implement moveCard(id, column, position) function that updates card position and reorders siblings using fractional indexing algorithm. Handle edge cases like moving to first/last position and between existing cards. Ensure position values maintain proper ordering.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test position calculations, verify card order is maintained after multiple moves, test edge cases for first/last positions

subtasks.title: Add comment operations with proper card relationships
subtasks.description: Implement comment CRUD operations with proper foreign key relationships to cards
subtasks.details: Create createComment(cardId, data) function that inserts comments with UUID and timestamp. Modify getCards() to include nested comments. Ensure cascade delete behavior when cards are deleted. Handle comment ordering and relationships properly.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test comment creation, verify nested comments in getCards(), test cascade delete behavior when parent card is deleted

subtasks.title: Optimize with prepared statements
subtasks.description: Enhance all database operations with better-sqlite3 prepared statements for improved performance
subtasks.details: Convert all database queries to use prepared statements for better performance and security. Create reusable prepared statement objects for frequently used queries. Implement proper statement cleanup and error handling.
subtasks.testStrategy: Performance benchmarks comparing prepared vs non-prepared statements, verify all operations still work correctly

subtasks.title: Add comprehensive error handling
subtasks.description: Implement robust error handling across all database operations with proper error types and messages
subtasks.details: Add try-catch blocks around all database operations. Create custom error types for different failure scenarios (not found, constraint violations, etc.). Ensure proper error propagation and logging. Handle database connection issues gracefully.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test error scenarios like invalid IDs, constraint violations, database connection failures, verify proper error messages and types

tasks.expansionPrompt: Break down into: 1) Implement basic CRUD operations (create, read, update, delete), 2) Implement complex moveCard function with fractional indexing, 3) Add comment operations with proper card relationships, 4) Optimize with prepared statements, 5) Add comprehensive error handling

tasks.title: Create REST API routes for cards
tasks.description: Implement Express routes for card CRUD operations
tasks.details: Create server/api/cards.js with Express router: GET /api/cards (calls getCards()), POST /api/cards (validates title required, calls createCard), PATCH /api/cards/:id (validates card exists, calls updateCard), DELETE /api/cards/:id (calls deleteCard). Add input validation middleware using express-validator or manual validation. Handle errors with proper HTTP status codes (400 for validation, 404 for not found, 500 for server errors). Mount router in server/index.js at /api path.
tasks.testStrategy: Integration tests using supertest covering happy path CRUD operations, 404 for unknown IDs, 400 for missing required fields

subtasks.title: Implement basic CRUD endpoints (GET, POST, PATCH, DELETE)
subtasks.description: Create the core Express router with basic CRUD operations for cards
subtasks.details: Create server/api/cards.js with Express router. Implement GET /api/cards endpoint that calls getCards() function. Add POST /api/cards endpoint that calls createCard() function. Implement PATCH /api/cards/:id endpoint that calls updateCard() function. Add DELETE /api/cards/:id endpoint that calls deleteCard() function. Set up basic route structure without validation or error handling.
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit tests for each endpoint to verify correct function calls and basic response structure

subtasks.title: Add input validation middleware and error handling
subtasks.description: Implement comprehensive input validation and error handling middleware
subtasks.details: Add input validation middleware using express-validator or manual validation. Validate required title field for POST requests. Validate card existence for PATCH and DELETE operations. Create error handling middleware to catch and format validation errors. Implement proper error response structure with meaningful error messages.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test validation errors return 400 status, missing required fields are caught, invalid IDs are handled

subtasks.title: Integrate with database query functions
subtasks.description: Connect API endpoints with database query functions from previous task
subtasks.details: Import and integrate getCards(), createCard(), updateCard(), and deleteCard() functions from the database layer. Ensure proper data flow between API endpoints and database operations. Handle database connection errors and query failures. Test integration between API layer and database layer.
subtasks.testStrategy: Integration tests to verify data persistence, database errors are handled gracefully

subtasks.title: Add comprehensive error responses with proper HTTP status codes
subtasks.description: Implement proper HTTP status codes and error response formatting
subtasks.details: Add proper HTTP status codes: 400 for validation errors, 404 for not found resources, 500 for server errors. Create consistent error response format with error messages and status codes. Mount the router in server/index.js at /api path. Add final error handling middleware for uncaught errors. Test all error scenarios return appropriate status codes.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test 404 for unknown card IDs, 400 for validation failures, 500 for server errors, verify error response format

tasks.expansionPrompt: Split into: 1) Implement basic CRUD endpoints (GET, POST, PATCH, DELETE), 2) Add input validation middleware and error handling, 3) Integrate with database query functions, 4) Add comprehensive error responses with proper HTTP status codes

tasks.title: Create REST API routes for comments
tasks.description: Implement Express routes for comment operations
tasks.details: Create server/api/comments.js with Express router: POST /api/cards/:id/comments (validates card exists, author and content required, calls createComment). Add validation for card existence before creating comment. Return 404 if card not found, 400 for missing fields. Mount router in server/index.js. Comments are fetched as part of getCards() so no separate GET endpoint needed.
tasks.testStrategy: Integration tests for comment creation, verify comments appear in card data, test validation errors

subtasks.title: Implement POST endpoint for comment creation
subtasks.description: Create the Express router and POST endpoint for creating comments on cards
subtasks.details: Create server/api/comments.js file with Express router. Implement POST /api/cards/:id/comments endpoint that extracts cardId from URL params, validates required fields (author and content), and calls the createComment database function. Set up proper request body parsing and response formatting.
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit tests for endpoint logic, verify correct parameters are passed to createComment function

subtasks.title: Add validation for card existence and required fields
subtasks.description: Implement comprehensive validation logic for comment creation requests
subtasks.details: Add middleware to validate that the card exists before creating a comment by checking the database. Validate that required fields (author and content) are present and not empty. Return appropriate HTTP status codes: 404 if card not found, 400 for missing or invalid fields. Include descriptive error messages in responses.
subtasks.testStrategy: Integration tests for validation scenarios, test 404 for non-existent cards, test 400 for missing fields

subtasks.title: Integrate error handling and mount router
subtasks.description: Add comprehensive error handling and integrate the comments router into the main application
subtasks.details: Implement try-catch blocks for database operations and other potential errors. Add proper error response formatting with consistent structure. Mount the comments router in server/index.js at the appropriate path. Ensure the router is properly integrated with existing middleware and error handling patterns.
subtasks.testStrategy: Integration tests for complete comment creation flow, verify router mounting works correctly, test error responses

tasks.details: Create client/src/api/client.js with functions: fetchCards() (GET /api/cards), createCard(data) (POST /api/cards), updateCard(id, data) (PATCH /api/cards/:id), deleteCard(id) (DELETE /api/cards/:id), createComment(cardId, data) (POST /api/cards/:id/comments). Add proper error handling, JSON parsing, and TypeScript-style JSDoc comments for better IDE support. Handle network errors and non-200 responses appropriately.

tasks.title: Implement useBoard hook for state management

Provide functions: createCard, updateCard, deleteCard, moveCard, addComment that call API and update local state optimistically. Handle loading states and errors. Structure state as {ready: [], in_progress: [], done: []} for easy column rendering.

Track reconnection attempts and reset counter on successful connection. Implement useRef for reconnection timeout and attempt counter. Add logic to automatically reconnect on connection loss unless explicitly disconnected by user. Prevent multiple simultaneous reconnection attempts.

subtasks.details: Add useEffect cleanup function that closes WebSocket connection and clears reconnection timeouts on component unmount. Clear all timeouts and intervals on cleanup. Handle edge cases like rapid connect/disconnect cycles and component re-renders.

Log renormalization events for monitoring. Test with many reorder operations to verify stability.

subtasks.title: Implement gap detection logic for fractional positions
subtasks.description: Add logic to detect when position gaps between adjacent cards become smaller than 0.001 threshold
subtasks.details: Enhance moveCard function in queries.js to check position differences between adjacent cards after each move operation. Calculate gaps between consecutive positions and trigger renormalization when any gap falls below 0.001. Include edge case handling for first/last positions in columns.
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit tests to verify gap detection triggers at correct threshold, test edge cases with single cards and boundary positions

subtasks.title: Create renormalization algorithm with database transactions
subtasks.description: Implement the core renormalization logic that resets all positions in a column to integers while maintaining order
subtasks.details: Create renormalizeColumn function that fetches all cards in a column ordered by position, reassigns integer positions (1, 2, 3, etc.), and updates database within a transaction. Ensure atomicity using database transactions to prevent data corruption during renormalization process.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test transaction rollback on failures, verify card order preservation, test concurrent access scenarios

subtasks.title: Add monitoring and performance testing for large datasets
subtasks.description: Implement logging for renormalization events and conduct performance testing with large card datasets
subtasks.details: Add comprehensive logging for renormalization events including column ID, card count, and execution time. Create performance tests with datasets of 1000+ cards to verify renormalization completes efficiently. Monitor memory usage and database lock duration during operations.
subtasks.testStrategy: Performance benchmarks with varying dataset sizes, stress testing with rapid reorder operations, monitoring log output verification

tasks.expansionPrompt: Split into: 1) Implement gap detection logic for fractional positions, 2) Create renormalization algorithm with database transactions, 3) Add monitoring and performance testing for large datasets

tasks.title: Add production optimizations and error handling
tasks.description: Implement production-ready error handling, logging, and performance optimizations
tasks.details: Add comprehensive error handling middleware to Express app. Implement request logging with morgan. Add rate limiting for API endpoints. Optimize client bundle with code splitting and lazy loading. Add error boundaries in React components. Implement proper loading states throughout the application. Add health check endpoint with database connectivity test. Configure proper CORS for production.
tasks.testStrategy: Test error scenarios are handled gracefully, performance meets requirements, health checks work correctly

subtasks.title: Implement comprehensive error handling middleware
subtasks.description: Create centralized error handling middleware for Express application to catch and process all errors consistently
subtasks.details: Create server/middleware/errorHandler.js with comprehensive error handling middleware. Handle different error types (validation, database, authentication, etc.) with appropriate HTTP status codes. Log errors with stack traces in development and sanitized messages in production. Return consistent error response format with error codes and user-friendly messages. Add 404 handler for unmatched routes.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test various error scenarios including validation errors, database errors, and unhandled exceptions to ensure proper error responses and logging

subtasks.title: Add logging and monitoring with morgan
subtasks.description: Implement request logging using morgan middleware and set up monitoring infrastructure
subtasks.details: Install and configure morgan for HTTP request logging. Set up different log formats for development (detailed) and production (combined). Configure log rotation and file output for production. Add custom logging utility for application events. Implement structured logging with timestamps, request IDs, and correlation tracking. Set up log aggregation strategy for production monitoring.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify request logs are generated correctly, log rotation works, and custom application logs are properly formatted and stored

subtasks.title: Configure rate limiting and security measures
subtasks.description: Implement API rate limiting and additional security middleware to protect against abuse
subtasks.details: Install express-rate-limit and configure rate limiting for API endpoints with different limits for different routes. Add helmet middleware for security headers. Implement IP-based rate limiting with Redis store for production scalability. Configure different rate limits for authenticated vs anonymous users. Add request size limits and timeout configurations. Implement graceful rate limit responses with retry-after headers.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test rate limiting triggers correctly, security headers are present, and rate-limited requests receive appropriate responses with retry information

subtasks.title: Optimize client bundle with code splitting
subtasks.description: Implement code splitting and lazy loading to optimize client-side bundle size and performance
subtasks.details: Configure Vite for code splitting and dynamic imports. Implement lazy loading for React components using React.lazy() and Suspense. Split vendor libraries into separate chunks. Optimize bundle size with tree shaking and dead code elimination. Add preloading strategies for critical routes. Configure chunk naming and caching strategies for optimal browser caching.
subtasks.testStrategy: Analyze bundle size reduction, verify lazy loading works correctly, test loading performance with network throttling, and ensure proper chunk caching

subtasks.title: Add React error boundaries and loading states
subtasks.description: Implement error boundaries and comprehensive loading states throughout the React application
subtasks.details: Create ErrorBoundary component to catch and handle React component errors gracefully. Implement loading states for all async operations (API calls, lazy-loaded components). Add skeleton screens and loading spinners with consistent styling. Create error fallback components with retry mechanisms. Implement global loading state management. Add error recovery strategies and user-friendly error messages.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test error boundaries catch component errors, loading states display correctly during async operations, and error recovery mechanisms work as expected

subtasks.title: Configure production CORS and health checks
subtasks.description: Set up production-ready CORS configuration and comprehensive health check endpoints
subtasks.details: Configure CORS middleware with production-specific origins, methods, and headers. Implement comprehensive health check endpoint that tests database connectivity, WebSocket server status, and external dependencies. Add readiness and liveness probes for container orchestration. Configure health check caching and timeout handling. Add metrics endpoint for monitoring system performance and resource usage.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test CORS configuration blocks unauthorized origins, health checks accurately report system status, and monitoring endpoints provide useful metrics

tasks.expansionPrompt: Break down into: 1) Implement comprehensive error handling middleware, 2) Add logging and monitoring with morgan, 3) Configure rate limiting and security measures, 4) Optimize client bundle with code splitting, 5) Add React error boundaries and loading states, 6) Configure production CORS and health checks
